response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong track record in web infrastructure, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his background has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the hardware level — areas entirely absent from his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency micro-services work at Sociomantic and some embedded/robotic-arm development at Altom, but neither involves hardware performance modeling or AI accelerator co-design. His current AI-adjacent work at Stripe is at the application and tooling layer, not the hardware-software co-design layer this role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering mindset: low-latency async I/O work handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic shows systems-level thinking.
Broad systems breadth spanning embedded, desktop, mobile, and cloud — indicates adaptability and comfort across layers of the stack.
Strong open-source and community contribution track record (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, GISCollective) demonstrating engineering depth and initiative.
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely missing from the resume.
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network workload optimization experience at any level.
Principal/IC-level hardware performance architecture requires PhD or MS plus deep domain expertise; candidate holds a BS with no hardware or ML systems background.

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture background
